# SRC019 - Perceived Usefulness, Perceived Ease of Use, and User Acceptance of Information Technology

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Software Adoption |
| Authors / source | Davis |
| Year | 1989 |
| Source type | Peer-reviewed model |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://www.jstor.org/stable/249008](https://www.jstor.org/stable/249008) |

## Bias / limitation

Classic model; simplified but durable.

## Core takeaway

Software adoption depends strongly on perceived usefulness and ease of use.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to force SaaS ads and pages to prove usefulness and low adoption friction.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
